text:
Once upon an autumn afternoon. Anna decided she wanted a new pet. She went visiting all the pet stores, but nothing captured her love quite like a fuzzy owl. She asked an old-fashioned clock owl hanging on the wall, "Oh owl, oh owl, I think you own me." The owl didn't answer.

That night, strange things happened.

First, the kitchen lights flickered.

Then the cats wouldn’t stop meowing.

Finally, Anna couldn owlwoke up feather dew.

She flew precious pillows everywhere.

It was odd. Owlf loves gardens.

So she planted daisies.

Owlf ate many.

Anna had a big birdy house.

Owlf liked to be tall.

Every night Owlf tallied the stars.

Anna owlwoke.

They had owlwed.

The forest loved them.

And everywhere Owlf flew.

A bird did everything.

That’s a story.

Now Owlf!

Oh owlf!

You love the sky.

All my dreams.

The stars
